decision_note: merge cayenne naming into the main capsicum family | merge cayenne-pepper naming into the main capsicum family | promote strong medicinal plant family from review into the seed shortlist

overview.title: The new family herbal : comprising a description and the medical virtues of British and foreign plants, founded on the works of eminent modern English and American writers on the medical properties of herbs; to which is added, the botanic family physician; valuable medical receipts; and important directions regarding diet , clothing, bathing, air, exercise, &c., &c. / by Matthew Robinson.
overview.excerpt: Many eminent physicians have great confidence in it, joined with sulphuric acid, and the tincture of cayenne pepper, in cases of phthisis, scrofula, ill-conditioned ulcers, rickets, scurvy, and in states of convalesce...

overview.title: Dental medicine, a manual of dental materia medica and therapeutics / by Ferdinand J.S. Gorgas.
overview.excerpt: In excessive doses, capsicum is an irritant poison. Therapeutic Uses. —It is employed in certain forms of dys- pepsia, flatulent colic, scarlet fever, yellow fever, delirium tremens, opium habit, etc. , etc. Externall...

overview.excerpt: cations are often beneficial; and in the cold stage of cholera, where frictions are indicated, cayenne pepper is often added to the ointments used for this purpose. It is a common adjunct to the Unguentum hydrargyri i...

retrieval_text: Capsicum is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 377 works and 1537 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Capsicum, Cayenne, Cayenne-pepper, Capsicums. Common plant-part co-mentions include Seed, Root, Bark, Gum, Leaf, Berry. Common preparation co-mentions include Tincture, Tea, Powder, Oil, Infusion, Emetic. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Wounds or ulcers, Fever, Vomiting or nausea, Rheumatism, Inflammation. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Toxicity watch, Allergy or sensitivity caution, Bleeding caution. Representative source works include The American vegetable practice, or a new and improved guide to health, designed for the use of families : in six parts / by Morris Mattson.; The American vegetable practice : or a new and improved guide to health, designed for the use of families : in six parts; The American vegetable practice, or a new and improved guide to health, designed for the use of families ... / By Morris Mattson.; The American family physician, or, Domestic guide to health : prepared expressly for the use of families, in language adapted to the understanding of the people : arranged in two divisions. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
